title: Fermented Festive Irish Rice Stew
description: A creamy, tangy vegetarian Irish stew that blends fermented cabbage and apple juice with brown rice for a festive, comforting bowl.
instructions: Rinse the brown rice under cold water until clear, then set aside.
Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add diced onion, carrot, and celery; sauté 5–7 minutes until softened.
Add minced garlic and smoked paprika, cooking 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in fermented apple juice to deglaze the pot, stirring to lift browned bits from the bottom.
Add drained fermented cabbage and stir well.
Pour in vegetable broth and bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 40 minutes, stirring occasionally.
After 35 minutes, add the rinsed rice, cover again, and continue simmering until the rice is tender, about 5–10 minutes more.
Season with salt and pepper to taste. Remove the pot from heat.
Serve hot, garnishing with fresh parsley if desired (optional).
